reasoning: The stock has experienced a massive run-up (over 100% YTD) and reached an intraday all-time high of $214.78 around October 24, but its all-time closing high was recorded at $206.77 on October 20. This suggests an intraday reversal or 'blow-off top', indicating rejection at higher prices and likelihood of short-term profit-taking. Additionally, the stock is currently trading well above the median analyst price targets published in September ($160-$185), signaling overvaluation relative to consensus fundamental models. For these reasons, the short-term probability of being strictly higher than the October 26 reference price (which corresponds to the October 24 close) is below 0.5. As the forecast horizon increases to November and January, strong underlying fundamentals (sold-out HBM, strong FY2026 guidance) combined with typical long-term stock volatility will cause the probability to gradually regress toward maximum uncertainty (0.5), in line with the required increasing uncertainty over time.
